title: Heteropappus distortus
taxonomy: Heteropappus distortus is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Heteropappus. The classification is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, clade: Asterids, order: Asterales, family: Asteraceae, genus: Heteropappus, species: H. distortus. uses: Heteropappus distortus, also known as the Chinese fleabane, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Heteropappus distortus.
